title: linum austriacum subsp. mauritanicum
intro: Linum austriacum subsp. mauritanicum, also known as yellow flax or Mauritanian flax, is a subspecies of Linum austriacum, a flowering plant in the family Linaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of mucilages, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the skin and mucous membranes. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, thanks to its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Linum austriacum subsp. mauritanicum is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
